<!-- LLM-maintained catalog. Auto-regenerated by the /super-manus:impl
     phase-close promote gate after every accepted wiki promotion. Do NOT
     hand-edit beyond the H1 title and this header — your edits will be
     overwritten the next time a rule is promoted to wiki/<topic>.md.

     If you need to edit a rule, edit the topic file directly
     (wiki/<topic>.md). The next promote will re-scan all topic files and
     rebuild this index from their H1 + H2 headings.

     Schema (v0.9.8 R16, headings stable — parsed by sm_load_wiki and
     /super-manus:wiki-lint):
       - H1: "# Wiki index"
       - H2 per topic file: matches the topic file's H1 (e.g. `## runtime`
         under wiki/runtime.md whose H1 is `# Runtime`)
       - Bulleted list under each H2: one bullet per H2 rule heading in the
         topic file, format:
         `- [<rule heading>](<topic>.md#<anchor>) — <one-line summary>`

     The boundary between wiki and spec is documented in CLAUDE.md (`Wiki vs
     spec` decision tree). Short version: spec answers "what THIS module
     does", wiki answers "how WE write code in this project". -->
# Wiki index

Cross-module engineering rules. Architect, test-writer, code-writer, and
reviewer all read this before drafting / reviewing code. Promoted from
`docs/super-manus/impl/<module>/<update>/findings.md ## Reflections` via the
reviewer's `wiki-candidates:` flag at pre-close + user accept gate (see
`/super-manus:impl` phase-close promote gate).

<!-- No topic sections yet. The first promote will create
     wiki/<topic>.md and add a `## <topic>` section here. -->
